title: Issue 001 - Welcome
subject: Welcome to Chévere. 🤍
preview_text: A thoughtful place for culture, style, books, film, travel, and everything worth discovering.
note_from_sabrina:
Hi,

I'm so happy you're here.

Thank you for subscribing to The Chévere Letter.

I started Chévere because I wanted to create the kind of place I was always searching for—a thoughtful corner of the internet where books, films, travel, food, style, and culture all come together.

Every Friday you'll receive a curated edit filled with recommendations, stories, and discoveries that I genuinely think are worth your time.

Think of this as your favorite magazine arriving in your inbox every week.

No endless scrolling.

Just good finds.

I'm so glad you're here.

— Sabrina

recommendations.text: New here? Learn why I created Chévere, what the name means, and what you can expect every Friday.
recommendations.title: Read Our Story

recommendations.text: Whether you're looking for literary fiction, thrillers, romance, or fantasy, here's a curated list of books worth adding to your shelf.
recommendations.title: Build Your Summer Reading List

recommendations.text: Travel and street-style guide inspired by Dua's vacation energy: Mediterranean destinations, city-break outfits, beach-to-dinner and hot-girl itinerary ideas.
recommendations.title: How to vacation like Dua Lipa: A Summer travel Moodboard

featured_preview: There's something about summer that makes everything feel a little more indulgent — the shows are bigger, the books are steamier, and the snacks require a picnic blanket. Here's what's currently living in my rotation.
obsessed_text: Every Sunday lately has looked the same in the best way: I catch up on the latest episode of The Therapuss Podcast by Jake Shane (which airs every Wednesday), followed by House of the Dragon on HBO, in which a new episode airs every Sunday. It's become my favorite way to slow down, reset for the week ahead, and end the weekend with something worth talking about.
weekend_text: Make a fresh summer tomato pasta, then head out for a long walk, whether that's through your favorite city neighborhood or around your local suburb. Wander without a destination, stop into a coffee shop you've never tried, browse a small independent bookstore, or simply see what you discover along the way. Sometimes the best weekends don't need a plan.
last_thing:
This week's reminder

Curiosity makes life bigger.

Stay curious this weekend—you never know what you'll discover.

Until next Friday,
Stay Chévere,
Sabrina
